meta.name: Lemon Pound Cake (Dense Loaf Cake, Optional Glaze)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A classic lemon pound cake loaf: dense yet tender crumb with bright lemon flavor. Built for clean slicing and make-ahead storage. Includes commercial flour or fine fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one). Optional lemon glaze included.

ingredients.label: Sour cream or plain Greek yogurt
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. Adds tenderness and moisture.

ingredients.label: Commercial flour option (use this OR the fresh-milled option): all-purpose flour
Fresh-milled flour option (use this OR the commercial option): fine-milled, sifted
ingredients.notes: Sift coarse bran for a smoother crumb. Fresh-milled absorbs more; batter may need +15–30 ml extra milk if thick.

ingredients.label: Extra milk (fresh-milled adjustment)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if batter is overly thick; up to ~45 ml total.

steps.title: Prep oven and pan
steps.body: Heat oven to 325°F (165°C). Line a 9x5 loaf pan with parchment with overhang and lightly grease. Pound cake benefits from a slightly lower bake temperature for even cooking.

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the commercial flour option OR the fresh-milled flour option (not both). If using fresh-milled flour, sift out coarse bran for a cleaner crumb.

steps.title: Rub zest into sugar (flavor extraction)
steps.body: In the mixer bowl, combine sugar and lemon zest. Rub together with fingertips for 30 seconds until fragrant (this releases lemon oils into the sugar).

steps.title: Cream butter and sugar
steps.body: Add butter to the lemon sugar. Beat 3–5 minutes until very light and fluffy, scraping bowl as needed. Proper creaming supports the dense-yet-tender structure.

steps.title: Add eggs gradually
steps.body: Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each and scraping bowl. If mixture looks slightly curdled, it will smooth out when flour is added.

steps.title: Add liquids
steps.body: Beat in lemon juice, sour cream/yogurt, milk, and vanilla (if using) until combined.

steps.title: Add dry ingredients (minimal mixing)
steps.body: Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt together. Add to batter and mix on low just until combined. Do not overmix.

steps.title: Adjust for fresh-milled flour (if needed)
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and batter is very thick/stiff, add extra milk 15 ml at a time until batter falls from the spatula in thick ribbons. Rest 5 minutes to hydrate, then mix briefly on low.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Scrape batter into pan and smooth top. Bake 60–80 minutes until deeply golden and a tester comes out clean or with a few moist crumbs. For accuracy, internal temp should be ~200–205°F (93–96°C). If top browns too quickly, tent with foil after ~45 minutes.

steps.title: Cool
steps.body: Cool in pan 15 minutes, then lift out and cool fully on a rack. Pound cake slices best once fully cool.

steps.title: Optional: glaze
steps.body: Whisk powdered sugar with lemon juice to a thick but pourable consistency. Stir in optional zest. Drizzle over cooled cake and let set 15–30 minutes before slicing.

notes: Dense loaf logic: thorough creaming and gentle final mixing create a tight, tender crumb without toughness.
Flavor logic: rubbing zest into sugar boosts lemon aroma throughout the loaf.
Fresh-milled flour: sift coarse bran and expect higher absorption; small milk adjustment and a short rest can improve texture.
Make-ahead: lemon pound cake improves after resting overnight (flavor blooms).

storage.refrigerator: Wrapped airtight: up to 6 days (bring to room temp for best texture).
storage.freezer: Freeze whole loaf or slices wrapped airtight up to 2 months.
